news.summary: A recent study revealed that over 90% of tested children displayed signs of exposure to toxic residue from tobacco smoke. The research found the highest levels of exposure among Black children and those residing in low-income and low-education households. This study, conducted in Cincinnati, highlights the concerning prevalence of tobacco smoke remnants in dust, old carpets, and furniture.
news.headline: Over 90% of children tested have toxic thirdhand smoke exposure, with higher levels in Black children and those in low-income households.

news.headline: Hospital PMI at 52% in May 2025, marking 21st consecutive month of expansion.

news.summary: The global market for demyelinating diseases therapeutics is growing significantly, valued at USD 25 billion in 2024 and projected to reach USD 45 billion by 2034, with a CAGR of 6.3% from 2025 to 2034. This growth is driven by the rising prevalence of neurological disorders like multiple sclerosis (MS), neuromyelitis optica (NMO), acute disseminated encephalomyelitis (ADEM), and chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (CIDP). The market is advancing through personalized medicine, biologics, and digital health integrations. Monoclonal antibodies, corticosteroids, and immunomodulators dominate the product types. MS is the largest disease segment, affecting around 2.8 million individuals globally, followed by NMO and CIDP. Disease-Modifying Therapies (DMTs) are crucial in managing these conditions. Biologics lead therapeutically, with adults comprising the majority of patients. North America holds a 45% market share, followed by Europe at 30%, while the Asia-Pacific region is the fastest-growing market with a projected CAGR of 8% through 2034. Scientific advancements, personalized medicine, regulatory developments, and increased healthcare spending are key market drivers.
news.headline: Global demyelinating diseases therapeutics market to hit USD 45 billion by 2034 at 6.3% CAGR. North America leads with 45% share, Europe follows with 30%. Asia-Pacific fastest-growing at 8% CAGR. Biologics top therapeutic class. Multiple Sclerosis largest disease segment. Adults mostly affected demographic. Growth driven by advancements in personalized medicine and regulatory developments.

news.summary: The European Medicines Agency's safety committee has completed a review of medicines containing semaglutide due to concerns of an increased risk of developing NAION, an eye condition that may lead to vision loss. Semaglutide, found in drugs like Ozempic, Rybelsus, and Wegovy, is a GLP-1 receptor agonist used for diabetes and obesity. The committee found NAION to be a very rare side effect of semaglutide, possibly affecting up to 1 in 10,000 individuals. Studies indicate a twofold higher risk of NAION in adults with type 2 diabetes using semaglutide compared to non-users. Novo Nordisk's stock (NYSE: NVO) might be impacted by this safety update. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: EMA's safety committee found Novo Nordisk's semaglutide may increase NAION risk, affecting up to 1 in 10,000 people.

news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4%, the lowest it has been in the past decade. The economy has added 250,000 new jobs in the last quarter alone, exceeding all expectations. Additionally, the stock market has seen a record-breaking increase, with the main index reaching an all-time high of 35,000 points. Experts believe that these positive indicators are a result of the government's recent economic policies and increased consumer confidence. Overall, these numbers reflect a strong and growing economy.
news.headline: Record high US household debt reaches new peak; Are you feeling the financial strain?

news.summary: Today, it was reported that the unemployment rate in the United States has decreased to 5.2% in the latest data, showing a significant improvement in job market conditions. The economy also added 943,000 jobs in the past month, surpassing expectations and indicating a strong recovery from the pandemic-induced downturn. The positive trend is further supported by an increase in average hourly earnings by 0.6% and a rise in labor force participation. These numbers signal a promising outlook for the US economy, although concerns remain about inflation and potential economic risks in the future.
news.headline: US job growth slowed in May with 139,000 payrolls added, signaling a gradual moderation in the economy.

news.summary: In Orlando, Florida, on June 6, 2025, Florida Realtors®, in conjunction with Realtors® across the U.S., are commemorating National Homeownership Month in June. This observance highlights the significance of homeownership and its influence on the lives of American citizens.
news.headline: Florida Realtors® celebrate National Homeownership Month in June, recognizing the importance of owning a home.

news.summary: Last week, the mayor announced that the city's budget deficit increased to $20 million due to unexpected costs in infrastructure repairs and increased spending on public services. In response to this, the city council approved a 10% reduction in funding for all departments, resulting in layoffs for 150 city employees. The mayor also proposed a tax increase of 2% to help cover the deficit. Additionally, the city plans to cut back on non-essential services to save $5 million by the end of the fiscal year. This financial crisis has prompted concerns among residents about the impact on essential services and the overall quality of life in the city.
news.headline: Markets rebound as US jobs report approaches; futures up.

The company's strong performance was attributed to its focused innovation and execution strategies, solidifying its position in the cyber resilience market. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Rubrik (NYSE: RBRK) exceeded Q1 expectations with a loss of 15 cents per share and revenue of $278.48 million. Analysts raised forecasts.

news.summary: In Stockholm on June 6, 2025, Sobi® (STO: SOBI) and Apellis Pharmaceuticals, Inc. revealed fresh findings from the open-label phase of the Phase 3 VALIANT trial. The study explores Aspaveli® (pegcetacoplan) for treating C3 glomerulopathy (C3G) and primary immune complex disorders. The data presented sheds light on the efficacy of Aspaveli® in managing these conditions. This development marks a significant step forward in the medical field's understanding of C3G and primary immune complex disorders.
news.headline: Sobi and Apellis show sustained one-year efficacy in Aspaveli trial for rare kidney diseases. #RareDisease #Phase3Study

news.summary: Soligenix, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company listed as Nasdaq: SNGX, based in Princeton, New Jersey, recently emphasized the contributions of Ellen Kim, M.D., who serves as the Lead Principal Investigator for their Phase 3 FLASH (1 and 2) studies. These studies focus on early-stage cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL), particularly mycosis fungoides (MF), which is the predominant type of CTCL. The company is dedicated to developing and launching products aimed at addressing rare diseases with unmet medical needs. This specific endeavor highlights the advancement of HyBryte™, a synthetic hypericin, as a potential novel therapy for individuals affected by MF.
news.headline: Soligenix highlights Dr. Ellen Kim's work on Phase 3 FLASH studies for HyBryte in CTCL patients - potential new therapy.

news.summary: 📊 The Monetary Authority of Singapore has implemented a ruling that restricts the majority of cryptocurrency companies from exclusively catering to overseas customers without a license. The authority stated that such licenses will be rarely issued.
news.headline: Singapore imposes strict regulations on foreign-only digital token services, requiring a license for operation, with few being issued.
